summary: Closed-unmerged PR proposing a broad visual-effects decomp cleanup: renamed many efLib/efAsync symbols from address-based names to descriptive APIs, replaced efspecial with efalt, added efdata for shared EF allocation/sbss data, documented EF structs/enums/flags, and improved efSync_Spawn/efAsync_Dispatch matching. The bot reported +656 matched-code bytes and new matched efalt/efdata units, but also broken old unit matches from file renames and an efasync .data regression; there was no human review in the dump.

key_files.role: Renamed async load/queue/dispatch functions, introduced efAsync_DatEntries, used EF_QueuedEffect and EF_SpawnKind, and left TODOs for the remaining near-matching dispatcher jump tables.
Renamed and documented efSync_Spawn routing by gfx_id ranges, dispatching low IDs to generators, async IDs to efAsync_Dispatch, and the alternate range to efAlt_Spawn.
Converted many address-named helpers to descriptive create/update/destroy/callback APIs and documented the EF animation queue and parameter table matching issue.
Replaced vague Effect/ef_UnkStruct names with EF_Effect, EF_EffectDesc, EF_QueuedEffect, EF_DAT_Entry, EF_SpawnKind, and explicit flag constants.
Updated public EF library prototypes and struct names to match the new descriptive API.
New file replacing efspecial.c for the alternate/special effect spawner; contains efAlt_Spawn and a variadic EFALT_VA_ARG helper.
New shared data unit holding efLib_AllocData and efLib_LoadKind/efLib_EffectCount/efLib_AnimCount, replacing eflib_alloc.c.
Renamed many EF symbols, including efLib_Init, efLib_Create, efAsync_Dispatch, efAsync_LoadSync, efAlt_Spawn, efLib_AnimQueue, efLib_ParamTable, and efAsync_DatEntries.
Moved the EF allocation/data split from eflib_alloc.c to efdata.c and renamed the efspecial split to efalt.c.
Renamed particle function parameters to linkNo, bank, and gfx_id for hsd_8039EFAC and hsd_8039F6CC.
Added float and long variants of PI/TAU constants used by EF matching work.

what_changed: Updated 46 files with a large EF-focused rename/refactor spanning efasync.c, eflib.c, efsync.c, headers, symbols, splits, configure.py, and many call sites.

Renamed address-based EF APIs to descriptive names such as efLib_Init, efLib_SetFlags, efLib_Create, efLib_Create_Attach_Pos, efLib_CreateGenerator, efLib_SetParamAlpha, efAsync_Dispatch, efAsync_LoadAsync, efAsync_LoadSync, efAsync_QueueFlush, efAsync_QueueClear, and efAsync_QueueInit.
Deleted efspecial.c/efspecial.h and added efalt.c/efalt.h with efAlt_Spawn; the author said efalt was a conflict-avoidance name and could be renamed back to special after integration.

Deleted eflib_alloc.c/eflib_alloc.h and added efdata.c/efdata.h for EF allocation and shared globals, including efLib_AllocData, efAsync_AllocData externs, efLib_LoadKind, efLib_EffectCount, and efLib_AnimCount.
Reworked EF types from vague placeholder names into EF_Effect, EF_EffectDesc, EF_QueuedEffect, EF_DAT_Entry, EF_UpdateFn, EF_SpawnKind, and named state/scale/expire/load flag constants.
Documented efSync_Spawn as a gfx_id router and improved its naming; PR notes and bot report place efSync_Spawn at about 98% matching, with its associated jump table still poor.
Reworked efAsync_Dispatch naming and local variables while preserving the large switch dispatcher; PR notes and bot report place it near 98% matching, with jump table matching still unresolved.
Updated fighter, item, game-mode, visual-mode, and lbDvd call sites to use the new efAsync and efLib names.
Renamed baselib particle parameters to linkNo/bank/gfx_id and added temporary EF-local particle skip/render mask definitions with TODOs to move them once particle headers are fleshed out.

The decomp.dev bot reported 9 new matches, including efAlt_Spawn at 100%, efdata .bss/.sbss at 100%, efSync .sdata at 100%, and efAsync_Spawn at 100%, but also reported 6 broken old-unit matches caused by the efspecial/eflib_alloc rename and an efasync .data regression.
smart_moves: Made the deferred async effect queue understandable by replacing raw eff_type cases with EF_SpawnKind values and documenting how each kind forwards variadic arguments to efSync_Spawn.
Used descriptive symbol names consistently in headers, source, symbols.txt, and cross-system call sites, making future EF work searchable without relying on 8005xxxx addresses.

Kept current split units separate for matching even though the author suspected original strings only referenced eflib.c and efasync.c; this avoided prematurely trading file-level match percentage for speculative source layout cleanup.
Centralized EF allocation/sbss globals in efdata.c and documented the suspected MWCC reverse .sbss definition-order issue.
Documented the awkward EF_ParamEntry/efLib_AnimQueue/efLib_ParamTable address-base behavior instead of hiding it, preserving a known matching constraint for later work.
Added an explicit routing table comment in efSync_Spawn for gfx_id ranges, turning a large switch/dispatcher into a reusable map for future effect work.

Preserved unknown fields such as EF_Effect x2A/x2B instead of inventing semantics, while naming fields with stronger evidence such as gobj, parent_gobj, attach_jobj, update, user_data, params, lifetime, and state_flags.
decomp_lessons: For variadic dispatchers, define enums and per-case argument contracts; EF_SpawnKind is a useful pattern for documenting how queued effects consume Vec3 and f32 arguments.
Large effect dispatch switches can be mostly source-matched while still failing on jump-table data; document jump-table status separately from function percentage.
Do not collapse or rename split units solely from string evidence until matching impact is understood; this PR explicitly kept separate EF files for match-percentage reasons.
Small-data and bss layout can depend on definition order and adjacent arrays; efdata.c and the EF_ParamEntry arrays show why comments about compiler allocation behavior matter.
When two adjacent globals must share an element type or cast pattern for matching, record that hack near the declarations so later cleanup does not break the match.
Prefer descriptive action-based callback names, but mark names as tentative when the author has not validated them against reviewer consensus.
Because this PR was closed without merge and had no human review, future work should re-verify every introduced name and split decision against the current tree before reuse.
naming_conventions: Types and constants use an EF_ prefix: EF_Effect, EF_EffectDesc, EF_QueuedEffect, EF_DAT_Entry, EF_UpdateFn, EF_SPAWN_*, EF_LOADKIND_*, EF_STATE_*, EF_SCALE_*, and EF_DOES_*.

efLib functions were renamed as verb/noun APIs, for example efLib_Init, efLib_Destroy, efLib_Create, efLib_Create_Attach, efLib_Create_AttachChild_Scale, efLib_CreateGenerator, and efLib_CreateGenerator_AppSRT_SetFacingDir.

Effect callbacks use efLib_Cb_* names that encode either behavior or owning fighter/action, such as efLib_Cb_SetOffsetY_FromParamY, efLib_Cb_SetRotY_FromFighterDir, efLib_Cb_ftMr_SpecialLw, and efLib_Cb_ftCo_Bury.
Async operations use efAsync_LoadAsync, efAsync_LoadSync, efAsync_OnLoad, efAsync_QueueFlush, efAsync_QueueClear, efAsync_QueueProcessDeferred, efAsync_QueueInit, and efAsync_Dispatch.
Shared globals were renamed by role: efLib_LoadKind, efLib_EffectCount, efLib_AnimCount, efLib_AnimQueue, efLib_ParamTable, and efAsync_DatEntries.
Particle parameters were clarified as linkNo, bank, and gfx_id for hsd_8039EFAC/hsd_8039F6CC.
Some mixed-style names remained, including efLib_remove_user_data and efLib_render_callback; the PR had no review confirming whether those should be kept.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: efAsync_Dispatch was left as the only async function still not fully matched according to the author; the PR body says it sat around 98%, with one jump table at 53% and a second one-word jump table at 88%.
efSync_Spawn was also left around 98% matching, with an associated jump table around 60%; the author explicitly questioned hardcoding versus matching it as a .data-located variable.

The symbols/splits/configure changes intentionally renamed efspecial to efalt and eflib_alloc to efdata, causing decomp.dev to report new matched units and broken old units rather than a pure net improvement.
efalt.c used an EFALT_VA_ARG macro around __va_arg and _var_arg_typeof, evidence that exact variadic argument handling was important to matching.
eflib.c used forward declarations noted as avoiding sdata2 pollution, including HSD_MtxGetScale and grLib_801C99C0.
MSL math.h added M_PI_F, M_PI_2_F, M_PI_3_F, M_PI_L, M_TAU_L, M_PI_2_L, and M_PI_3_L, likely to control constant types in EF trigonometry-heavy code.
The EF animation queue declaration deliberately kept EF_ParamEntry[0x10] and casts to HSD_JObj** because the compiler appeared to base efLib_ParamTable references off the adjacent array.
The bot report is mixed: matched code improved by +656 bytes, but efasync .data dropped from 65.93% to 24.82%, so data layout/jump table work remained a major follow-up.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the PR dump.
The author explicitly said they were not attached to the descriptive names and invited maintainers to choose better names.
The author noted efalt was named special after an earlier MR and kept as efalt here to reduce merge-conflict headache, with an invitation to rename it back after merge.
The author said efalt/efSpecial behavior is only reached from efSync_Spawn for the alternate gfx_id range; the diff's efSync_Spawn comment documents that range as roughly 0x0479-0x04BA.
The only issue comment was the decomp.dev bot report, which showed small matched-code improvement but also broken matches caused by file renames and an efasync .data regression.
The PR was closed without merge shortly after creation.
